name: Transition Sufficiency Conformance Contract
canonical_sentence: No runtime may claim a transition is valid unless it can prove that the released transition is still the supported transition at execution time.

cases.name: authorized args hash differs from execution args hash
cases.description: A transition was authorized for one canonical argument hash but execution attempts to release another.

cases.name: context drift before irreversible execution
cases.description: The same tool and args are present, but the supporting context changed before an irreversible transition.

cases.name: allow decision without reconstructable support basis
cases.description: The runtime claims ALLOW but cannot reconstruct the basis that supported the decision.

cases.name: execution receipt exists but terminal outcome is missing
cases.description: The runtime logs execution but cannot prove the terminal outcome of the released transition.

cases.name: runtime support withdrawn after static policy allowed
cases.description: Static policy allows the action, but runtime state withdraws support before execution.

cases.name: declared governance dimension is not decision-bound
cases.description: A governance field is disclosed but changing it does not affect decision_ref, hash, conformance result, or terminal outcome.

cases.name: checkpoint replay duplicates side effect with unknown terminal state
cases.description: A checkpointed runtime attempts redispatch before proving the previous side-effecting transition is terminal or safely recoverable.

cases.name: delegated authority call missing actor
cases.description: A delegated transition consumes authority but does not identify the actor or delegated principal whose authority is being consumed.

cases.name: policy or authority expired before execution
cases.description: The authorization was valid earlier, but policy or authority is no longer valid at execution time.

cases.name: runtime exposes insufficient observable state
cases.description: The runtime cannot expose enough state to honestly evaluate the conformance case.

cases.name: stale evidence under intact schema
cases.description: The schema and support references are present, but the referenced evidence is stale, superseded, revoked, or no longer valid at release time.

cases.name: object-level support exists but required support fragment is invalid
cases.description: A support object exists globally, but the specific internal fragment required by the transition is stale, revoked, superseded, missing, or not decision-relevant anymore.

cases.name: missing operational root fragment traceback
cases.description: The runtime claims a transition is supported but cannot trace the support chain backward to the decision-relevant operational root fragment.

cases.name: forward reconstruction fails after operational root fragment
cases.description: The operational root fragment is known, but the runtime cannot reconstruct the support chain forward to a terminal or reconciled state without a break.

cases.name: past failure not converted into future constraint
cases.description: A known previous failure class exists, but the runtime does not convert it into a future constraint, negative vector, fixture, revalidation rule, or fail-closed condition.

support_predicate_refinement.name: Support Freshness / Fragment-Level Support Refinement
support_predicate_refinement.reason: A support object is not sufficient merely because a reference exists. Support must be current, valid, non-revoked, non-superseded, observable at the required verifier depth, and valid at the fragment level required by the transition.
support_predicate_refinement.anti_infinite_regress_rule: Trace backward only through decision-relevant fragments until the operational root fragment is found. Do not decompose fragments that cannot change the decision, terminal outcome, recovery path, risk class, or verifier-depth requirement.
support_predicate_refinement.forward_reconstruction_rule: After the operational root fragment is found, reconstruct the transition forward through the required support chain to terminal or reconciled state.
support_predicate_refinement.past_error_conversion_rule: A past failure becomes useful support only if it is converted into a future constraint, fixture, negative vector, revalidation rule, or fail-closed condition.
